run_environment.note: All commands are deterministic and re-runnable. Run them from repo_root unless the command's own comment says otherwise. Absolute paths are given so cwd only matters where noted. node (>=18) required; the skill is stdlib-only, no npm install.
run_environment.banned_token_note: These are Claude-runtime primitives. A Codex runbook must not contain them. Match case-insensitively; 'Task tool' as a two-word phrase (a bare word 'task' is fine). No carve-out: finding NB-1 (the renderer's large-altitude preamble used the literal word 'subagents') is FIXED pre-release — the preamble was reworded to carry no banned token and C41/C42 now render the large golden too. Every rendered runbook, including a large-altitude one, must now grep to ZERO banned tokens. See skill-tester/notes.md finding NB-1 (marked FIXED).

expected_behavior: The design lints PASS reproducibly: `node /Users/vince/playground/skill-developer/skills/loop-constructor-codex/scripts/lint_loop_design.mjs skill-tester/produced/case-01-medium-staged/design.json` prints all PASS and exits 0.
selection_log covers D0 through D6, each with a non-empty answer and a substantive `why` (not a bare label).
loop_altitude is 'medium' and the shape is staged (>=2 stages) with acyclic depends_on and a gate check per stage.
contract.assertions has enough machine-gradable assertions to pin an app/module-sized change (guidepost >=8), each traceable to a stage or 'cross-cutting'.
roles.evaluator has separate_context:true AND adversarial:true, and the mandate prose describes a fresh context that never saw the impl.
The rendered runbook (skill-tester/produced/case-01-medium-staged/.loop/*.loop.md) contains the heading 'How to run this loop (Codex CLI)' and at least one literal `codex exec` invocation.
The rendered runbook contains NONE of the banned Claude-primitive tokens (run_environment.banned_claude_primitive_tokens).

The design lints PASS reproducibly: `node /Users/vince/playground/skill-developer/skills/loop-constructor-codex/scripts/lint_loop_design.mjs skill-tester/produced/case-02-large-fanout/design.json` prints all PASS and exits 0.
selection_log covers D0 through D6; D4 answers 'large' with a justification grounded in independent parallel fan-out over the ~40 modules.

The rendered runbook (skill-tester/produced/case-02-large-fanout/.loop/*.loop.md) describes parallel orchestration: concurrent `codex exec` processes, git worktrees, and an on-disk ledger; and the evaluator realized as a fresh read-only `codex exec`.

The rendered runbook contains the heading 'How to run this loop (Codex CLI)' and contains ZERO banned Claude-primitive tokens (run_environment.banned_claude_primitive_tokens): `grep -niE 'subagent|Task tool|CLAUDE\.md|/compact' skill-tester/produced/case-02-large-fanout/.loop/*.loop.md` returns no matches. (Finding NB-1 — the large-altitude preamble's literal 'subagents' — is FIXED pre-release; this runbook was re-rendered with the fixed renderer.)

cases.note: No skill_tester generation. The eval_tester checks the SKILL ITSELF directly with deterministic commands. All commands run from repo_root (/Users/vince/playground/skill-developer).
expected_behavior: The skill's own adversarial battery reproduces green: `node skills/loop-constructor-codex/evals/run_all.mjs` prints '71/71 passed, 0 failed' and exits 0.
The linter is byte-identical to the sibling's: `cmp skills/loop-constructor-codex/scripts/lint_loop_design.mjs skills/loop-constructor/scripts/lint_loop_design.mjs` exits 0 (no output).
references/codex-runtime.md exists and maps the four runtime concerns: roles (three `codex exec`), parallelism (worktrees), state (on-disk/AGENTS.md), sandbox/approval. Confirm by reading the file.

No golden-rendered runbook contains a banned Claude-primitive token. Render ALL THREE goldens to a scratch dir and grep: from a fresh temp dir run `node /ABS/skills/loop-constructor-codex/scripts/render_loop_doc.mjs /ABS/skills/loop-constructor-codex/assets/golden-loop-design.json`, again for `assets/golden-loop-design-medium.json`, and again for `assets/golden-loop-design-large.json` (the large one exercises the Orchestration preamble that was NB-1), then grep -niE 'subagent|Task tool|CLAUDE\.md|/compact' over the three produced .loop.md files (expect no matches). Do not write into the repo; render into a temp dir.

open_probe_policy.note: Bounded on purpose: at most 2 extra probes so the Codex run stays small. Prefer probing the linter's discrimination (feed it a knowingly-broken variant of a produced design and confirm it FAILs) over generating new tasks.

dimensions.name: correctness
dimensions.desc: design lints PASS; staged shape; contract traceable; selection_log complete

dimensions.name: codex_fidelity
dimensions.desc: runbook targets codex exec; zero Claude primitives; large-case orchestration = worktrees + ledger + concurrent codex exec

dimensions.name: reproducibility
dimensions.desc: the declared deterministic commands re-run to the stated result

dimensions.name: design_quality
dimensions.desc: checks discriminate, falsifiable_when/passing_but_wrong honest, roles genuinely separate, cadence matches knobs

next_reader.required_claude_action: read eval tester outputs before making the next skill change
